message:
fix: export TARGET_USER/TARGET_HOME before preflight subprocess (#243)

The preflight.sh disk space check (fixed in 67453e6e) correctly uses
TARGET_USER to find the install partition, but install.sh only exported
these variables in init_target_paths() — which runs AFTER the preflight
subprocess. The subprocess couldn't see TARGET_USER, so it fell back to
"ubuntu" and potentially checked the wrong partition.

Export both variables immediately after they're set so the preflight
subprocess inherits the correct target user for disk-space checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -145,10 +145,14 @@ if [[ -z "${TARGET_USER:-}" ]]; then
     TARGET_USER="$_ACFS_DETECTED_USER"
 fi
 unset _ACFS_DETECTED_USER
+# Export TARGET_USER early so subprocesses (e.g. preflight.sh) can use it
+# to determine the correct installation partition for disk-space checks (#243).
+export TARGET_USER
 # Leave TARGET_HOME unset by default; init_target_paths will derive it from:
 # - $HOME when running as TARGET_USER
 # - /home/$TARGET_USER otherwise
 TARGET_HOME="${TARGET_HOME:-}"
+export TARGET_HOME
 
 # Colors
 RED='\033[0;31m'
